title: quercus × diversiloba
intro: Quercus × diversiloba, also known as Japanese pedunculate oak, is a hybrid plant species that combines characteristics of the Japanese oak (Quercus acutissima) and the pedunculate oak (Quercus robur). The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus × diversiloba include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional East Asian medicine to treat various ailments.
